title: Lokasenna
stanzas: The gods made merry at Aegir's hall,
And Loki came among them there,
But he was not welcome in that great feast,
For he was the cause of much evil and pain.
Then Loki began with words of spite,
To mock each god with jibes and jeer,
He spoke of their deeds both foul and base,
And made them all grow red with shame.
To Odin he said, 'Thou dost wander far,
And takest the forms of beasts and men,
But thou art cowardly, a thief in the dark,
Who hides from the face of honest foes.'
To Thor he cried with bitter words,
'Thy strength is great, but thy wit is small,
I have made thee look the fool many times,
And thou didst never know my tricks.'
He mocked at Freya with words of scorn,
And said that all of the gods had known,
The touch of her hand in secret nights,
When she did walk without shame or fear.
He spoke of Sif with bitter jests,
'How much of thy wife's fair locks remain?'
And Thor grew red with anger dire,
But Loki only laughed the more.
Then Heimdall rose and faced the imp,
And told him that he was vile and base,
'Both of us were born from the same old sow,
But I have lived in honor and shame is thine.'
But Loki spoke with cunning words,
'Thou art not my equal nor my peer,
Thou livest on the bridge in the cold and rain,
Whilst I do feast with the gods above.'
At last the gods grew tired of his scorn,
And Heimdall struck him with mighty blow,
And Loki was bound beneath the earth,
With venom dripping upon his face.
There he shall remain in pain and torture,
Until the day of the great Ragnarok,
When all the bonds shall break at last,
And he shall break forth in fury dire.
